Service menu & rough guide pricing

Below is the StudyStats service menu — a transparent reference for the kinds of deliverables we produce and what each typically costs. Use it to estimate your project. Every engagement is then individually scoped, priced and invoiced based on your specific data, objectives and timeline.

All prices in ZAR, exclusive of VAT. Effective May 2026. Indicative only — see disclaimer below.

Descriptive analysis & comparison

Tables, graphs and basic comparisons that describe your sample.

Item Description Price
Simple descriptive table Counts, percentages, means, medians. One table = descriptive statistics from the same denominator. R300
Simple graph Bar charts, histograms, x–y scatter plots. R300
Advanced descriptive table Stratified by groups, summary statistics across multiple variables. R500
Advanced visualisation Boxplots, violin plots, multi-panel figures, coefficient (forest) plots. R600
Data wrangling for descriptives Cleaning, merging or reshaping data (per dataset). R400

Simple modelling — correlations, odds ratios, risk ratios

Univariate models for unadjusted associations and effect sizes.

Item Description Price
Univariate regression model Linear, logistic, Poisson, Cox, etc. R600
Model diagnostics Residual plots, fit statistics, VIF, assumptions check. R400
Data wrangling for model Preparing the analysis dataset. R400

Modelling adjusted for confounders

Multivariable models with sensitivity analyses and full diagnostics.

Item Description Price
Multivariable regression model Linear, logistic, Poisson, Cox, etc. R900
Model diagnostics Residual plots, AIC/BIC, ROC, calibration. R500
Sensitivity analyses Alternative specifications, sub-group models. R500
Data wrangling for model Handling missingness, transformations, variable coding. R500

Writing & interpretation

Methodology, results and interpretation text — drop-in for your manuscript or thesis.

Item Description Price
Methodology section Clear description of methods used; adequate for short manuscript (100–200 words). R600
Interpretation of results Short narrative summary of findings — a starting point for the discussion, completed by the PI. R600
Formal results — descriptives Formal results writing of descriptive statistics (< 100 words). R500
Formal results — simple modelling Formal results writing of simple modelling statistics (< 100 words). R500
Formal results — complex modelling Formal results writing of complex modelling (< 100 words). R500

Diagnostic performance studies

For sensitivity / specificity, ROC and prediction-model accuracy work.

Item Description Price
Sensitivity & specificity Basic 2×2 table (test vs. gold standard) with confidence intervals. R500
Predictive values (PPV, NPV) Added calculation, with prevalence adjustment if needed. R400
ROC curve (single test/model) ROC curve and AUC with confidence intervals. R700
ROC curve comparison Compare AUCs of two or more tests / models. R900
Calibration / agreement Hosmer–Lemeshow, Brier score, kappa. R600
Decision curve analysis Net benefit curves (optional advanced). R900
Data wrangling for accuracy Preparing dataset, binary outcomes, thresholds. R500
Methodology write-up Clear explanation of diagnostic methods used. R600
Interpretation write-up Plain-language summary of accuracy results. R600
Formal results section Journal-ready tables and figures. R800

This menu is a rough guide. Every project is individually scoped and quoted based on the complexity of your data, the number of objectives and your timeline. Some items combine, some need extending, and some don’t apply. After our free 15-minute call you’ll receive an itemised written quote drawn from this menu — the prices on your quote are what you pay, full stop.

Revisions, updates & ad-hoc work

The first round of revisions on each deliverable is included free (within 14 days of delivery). Subsequent revisions, supervisor changes and reviewer responses are billed in 30-minute blocks at R400, tracked transparently and invoiced monthly.

Payment terms

50% deposit secures your project start; the balance is due on delivery. Quotes are valid for 30 days. EFT and PayFast accepted. Tax invoice issued on payment. StudyStats is a registered vendor with several South African universities — purchase-order-compliant invoicing available on request.

Common questions

Why per-deliverable instead of hourly?

Per-deliverable pricing maps directly to what you and your supervisor already think in — “Table 1, Table 2, regression model, methods section”. You see exactly what you’re paying for and there are no hourly surprises if a model takes longer to run.

What if my data needs more cleaning than expected?

The cleaning fee is set during the scoping call once we’ve seen the data. If reality differs from what we agreed, we re-quote before doing the extra work — never after.

Can I buy just one or two items?

Yes. Many clients only need a sample size calculation, an ethics statistics section, or a single table. Minimum invoice R500.

Do you charge for the discovery call?

No. The first 15-minute call is free. If you need a deeper working session before committing, that can be booked as a paid scoping consult — and the fee is credited to your project if you proceed within 30 days.

Can my institution pay directly?

Yes. We can issue purchase-order-compliant invoices and tax invoices on request, and are already onboarded with several South African universities.

Ready to scope your project?

Bring your research question and your deadline — we’ll send back an itemised quote drawn from this menu, tailored to what you actually need.

Book a free 15-min consult → See how it works
